source: User approved diff rows UT-001..UT-004 after stating umbrella tasks should not exist and sprints should be cohorts.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

summary: Accepted no-umbrella task invariant: tasks are bounded units of work; sprints group related tasks; enforce policy to prevent umbrella tasks.

diff_table.current_state: Roadmap can model umbrella tasks that coordinate or contain child tasks, e.g. TASK-077 acceptance references child tasks and sprint completion.
diff_table.desired_state: Disallow umbrella tasks. Every task must be a bounded unit of work with clear acceptance, verification, and non-overlapping scope. Tasks must not contain, depend on completing, or act as implementation evidence for other tasks.
diff_table.rationale: Task should be the atomic execution/validation unit. Umbrella tasks blur ownership, create conflicting boundaries, and duplicate sprint responsibility.
diff_table.risk: Medium: existing roadmap entries may need migration/cancellation/splitting; validation and task creation/update paths must reject umbrella patterns.

diff_table.current_state: Related task grouping may be represented by task hierarchy/umbrella semantics.
diff_table.desired_state: Use sprints only as cohort/grouping mechanism for related tasks. Sprint records may group tasks and track aggregate progress, but cannot substitute for task acceptance evidence or close child work.
diff_table.rationale: Cohorts belong at sprint layer; execution proof belongs at task layer.
diff_table.risk: Medium: sprint/task APIs and docs need clear separation.

diff_table.current_state: CodeWiki may warn about vague or broad tasks but does not enforce no-umbrella invariant consistently.
diff_table.desired_state: Enforce no-umbrella invariant in task creation/update/close validation and audits. Flag or block tasks whose title/summary/acceptance/non-goals/verification indicate coordination-only, parent/child closure, “umbrella”, or sprint-completion semantics.
diff_table.rationale: Policy must be executable to prevent recurrence.
diff_table.risk: Medium: heuristic enforcement can false-positive; tests need representative allowed bounded tasks and rejected umbrella tasks.

diff_table.current_state: TASK-077 is currently described as an umbrella sprint task and remains open.
diff_table.desired_state: Migrate TASK-077 away from umbrella semantics: close/cancel/decompose only through accepted feedback/planning; preserve validated implementation slice evidence separately; do not let TASK-077 continue as parent container.
diff_table.rationale: Dogfood state must comply with new invariant, but migration must not lose evidence for completed slices.
diff_table.risk: Medium: changing current roadmap state requires careful traceability and likely follow-up tasks/sprint adjustment.

accepted_decisions.summary: Tasks are the atomic unit of work and must have clear bounded scope.
Sprints are the cohort/grouping mechanism for related tasks.
Task creation/update/close and audits should enforce no-umbrella invariants.
Existing TASK-077 umbrella semantics must be migrated without losing completed slice evidence.

non_goals: Do not implement enforcement in this feedback step.
Do not close/cancel TASK-077 without documentation/planning traceability.

roadmap: task to enforce no-umbrella invariant and migrate TASK-077
validation: feedback acceptance for no-umbrella task invariant
